title: Level 0.11
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= /* DARK MODE */ .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: #05080f; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #05080f; --theme-page-text-color: #e8e8e8; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-link-color: #f47a20; --theme-accent-color: #6b6320; --theme-border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); --theme-body-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; } /* LIGHT MODE */ .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: #05080f; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #05080f; --theme-page-text-color: #e8e8e8; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-link-color: #f47a20; --theme-accent-color: #6b6320; --theme-border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); --theme-body-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; } html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Golos Text'; } }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 0.11: “Water Damage”}} {{Background|file=Idkwhatto.webp}}{{Class 4e}} '''Level 0.11''' is a sublevel located between [[Level 0]] and [[Level 1]]. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Idkwhatto.webp|caption=An image of '''Level 0.11'''}} '''Level 0.11''', commonly referred as '''Water Damage''' is an expansive non-euclidean complex of flooded concrete rooms and hallways. All documented rooms in '''Level 0.11''' share the same features, with decaying rotting carpets, dull concrete walls, ceramic ceilings, and inconsistent placed fluorescent lighting. Aside from these qualities, no two rooms in the level are the same, with each area differing significantly in shape and layout. Upon entering '''Level 0.11''', one may find it difficult to maintain an accurate sense of direction. This is a result of the level's tendency to inexplicably shift and change; this phenomenon of altering corridors often confuses and disorients wanderers. Rooms and corridors branch off in all directions, occasionally leading to dead-ends or looping in on themselves. '''Level 0.11''' is littered with various objects, such as papers scattered around the floors, wet floor signs, construction tools and wooden barrels. Aside from these objects, little to no food and water are found in '''Level 0.11'''. {{Blurimage|file=IMG_3684.webp|caption=A blue door located in '''Level 0.11'''}} The origin of '''Level 0.11’s''' architecture appears to be man-madeIt is evident as construction materials are scattered throughout the level.. Analysis concluded that the walls is made by greenish concrete decaying at a gradual, constant rate. The moist carpeting surfacing the floor is brown, sharply contrasting with the concrete walls and ceilings of '''Level 0.11'''. The lighting system of '''Level 0.11''' is relatively poor, habitually flickering and sometimes shutting off entirely. Furthermore, these panels negatively impact one's mental health, as staring into the light for extended periods may cause mental disorientation among wanderers. {{Blurimage|file=Uwuigidk.png|caption=Another image of '''Level 0.11'''}} The waters, noticeable for their greenish brown tint, surfaces the majority of '''Level 0.11''', with the water level ranging between 10-50 centimeters in height, occasionally fluctuating by tens of centimeters. The waters are unsafe with them infested by several pathogens and has a high concentration of sediments: including dirt, sand and silt. Some of the level’s areas are overgrown with algae and fungi, leaving obvious warnings to not go into the water. As such, it is not recommended to drink the water or come in contact with the water for extended periods of time as they may carry diseases or pathogens alike. As such, [[Phenomenon 24|Flash Floods]] may occasionally occur in '''Level 0.11''', thus further making the level more dangerous. Similar to [[Level 2]], a uniform network of rusty metal pipes line the walls. Opening the pipes reveals a greenish-brown water, pollutants that contribute to the smog in '''Level 0.11''', or a viscous substance similar to crude oil or tar. The pipes are the source of the greenish-brown water located in the surface of the water, although it is unknown where the water originates from. The pipes are heavily rusted, with them appearing dark brown. The pipes are also in low quality, leading them to occasionally break and release these substances. In addition, The pipes, as well as the liquid they contain, emits a repulsive smell similar to decaying flesh or rotting produce, among other things. {{Blurimage|file=0.11.webp|caption=A hallway branching off two separate directions in '''Level 0.11'''}} The obtrusive smog of '''Level 0.11''' contains significant levels of pollutants, such as carbon monoxide and various nitrogen oxides. Such contaminants commonly result from polluted waters, further suggesting some form of human occupation within the level, though this has yet to be proven. Additionally, a rotting smell perpetuates the entirety of '''Level 0.11''' as a result of the decaying walls and the pollutants of the level. '''Level 0.11''' is quieter than other sublevels of [[Level 0]], with the only ambient noise present being the constant dripping water and movement of the water. The enclosed structure of the level causes the sounds to have a light echo — making the sound universally heard across the entire level, regardless of one's location. Other than this, the only other naturally occurring sound within '''Level 0.11''' is the moderately loud rumbling of pipes, similar to what one would hear on [[Level 2]]. {{Blurimage|file=0.11.jpeg|caption=A picture of a Safe Zone in '''Level 0.11'''}} One may find dry and safe regions of the level, informally known as '''Safe Zones'''. These regions are often noticeable by the lack of water, worn wallpaper mimicking [[Level 0]] and the absence of pipes and pollutants. They are safer than the rest of the level as they do not include '''Skin-Stealers''' and has minimal environmental hazards. These regions are difficult to find throughout the level, as they blend in with the rest of the level and are relatively uncommon. ==Entities== '''Level 0.11''' does not present many entities in its surroundings, with the only two confirmed being '''Skin-Stealers''' and the [[Hydrolitis Plague]], a deadly disease that lurks around the water of '''Level 0.11'''. ==Symptoms== The following table lists possible symptoms experienced by wanderers staying in '''Level 0.11'''. {| class="wikitable" ! Time Spent in Level 0.11 ! Likely Symptoms |- | 0-60 minutes | Headache, dizziness, mild breathing discomfort |- | 1-3 hours | Nausea, worsening headache, increased confusion |- | 3-6 hours | Mental disorientation, psychological stress, difficulty focusing |- | 6-12 hours | Fatigue from breathing the rotting smell, risk of infection if exposed to water |- | 12-24 hours | Illness symptoms such as fever or stomach discomfort if water is consumed or contacted |- | 1-3 days | Severe exhaustion, worsening illness, difficulty thinking clearly |- | 3+ days | Severe sickness, possible infection, severe mental disorientation |} ==Addendum== {{M.E.G Notice}} {{Login|organization=M.E.G|username={{USERNAME}}|password=CheckOut[[Level 27|Level27]]!|body=
Addendum: "Tips For Survival"
{{M.E.G Notice1|notice=This is compiled by the Major Explorer Group (M.E.G.), therefore it is only accessible by [[M.E.G.]] members. Leaking this survival guide to other wanderers outside the [[M.E.G.]] or/and an unassociated organization will lead to disciplinary action.}} Level 0.11 is an environmentally hazardous sublevel located between [[Level 0]] and [[Level 1]]. Although the number of entities within the level is relatively low, the environment itself presents numerous dangers that can threaten the survival of wanderers. The flooded corridors, contaminated air, unstable architecture, and disorienting layout require careful navigation and constant awareness. The M.E.G. strongly recommends marking pathways when traveling through this level. Objects such as tools, debris, or paper found on the floor may be used as directional markers. Wanderers should avoid traveling too far from previously explored areas without a reliable method of tracking their movement. The water is unsafe for consumption and may cause illness if ingested. Wanderers should avoid allowing the water to contact open wounds or exposed skin for long periods of time. In addition, sudden fluctuations in water level have been reported. Flash flooding events may occasionally occur, posing a serious danger in lower areas of the level. Another major hazard present within Level 0.11 is the network of rusted metal pipes that line many of the walls. These pipes appear heavily corroded and may release contaminated fluids when damaged. The substances within these pipes are believed to contribute to the polluted water found throughout the level. The fluids may also emit a strong odor similar to rotting organic material. A persistent smell of decay is present throughout the level. This odor is believed to originate from the polluted water, decaying structures, and substances within the pipe system. Wanderers should avoid remaining in enclosed areas where the air appears especially dense or stagnant. Despite the hazardous conditions present in Level 0.11, some relatively safe areas do exist. These locations are informally referred to as '''Safe Zones'''. Safe Zones are typically dry areas where the flooded water is absent and the surrounding pipes are not present. If a Safe Zone is located, wanderers are advised to remain there temporarily in order to recover from exhaustion and limit exposure to the polluted environment. Level 0.11 contains relatively few confirmed entities. However, two notable threats have been documented within the level. The first are '''Skin-Stealers''', which may occasionally appear within flooded areas of the level. The second threat is the '''Hydrolitis Plague''', a deadly disease believed to exist within the contaminated waters of the level. Contact with infected water may increase the risk of contracting this illness. Wanderers should therefore minimize exposure to the water and remain vigilant while traveling through flooded corridors. === General Survival Recommendations === The M.E.G. recommends the following precautions when traveling through Level 0.11: * Avoid prolonged contact with the contaminated water. * Do not drink any liquid found within the level. * Mark pathways to prevent becoming lost in the shifting corridors. * Avoid staring at the flickering lights for extended periods. * Remain alert for signs of flash flooding. * Seek out Safe Zones whenever possible. Remaining calm and maintaining a clear sense of direction greatly increases the chances of surviving Level 0.11.
}} ==Colonies and Outposts== Despite '''Level 0.11''' not possessing [[Phenomenon 15]], the environmental hazards and entities prove to be a challenge of settling colonies and outposts, regardless safe regions being present in the level. However further research has concluded that human presence in '''Level 0.11''' is possible, as construction materials, tools and papers could be found throughout the level further hinting former inhabitation. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== '''Level 0.11''' has four prominent entrances: *A greenish, rusted door found in [[Level 0.1]] may seemingly transition into '''Level 0.11'''; *A blue door labeled No Access located in [[Level 0.8]] leads to '''Level 0.11'''; *Various sewers in [[Level 4]] may lead one to '''Level 0.11'''; *Venturing deeper in [[Level 34]] may transition into '''Level 0.11'''. ===Exits=== '''Level 0.11''' has three prominent exits: *Nocliping through a rare, beige wall may lead to [[Level 0.22]] *Entering a large, open glowing blue passage will lead out to [[Level 7.5]]. *Interacting with an extremely rare untextured block leads to [[Level 0.5]]. {{Redirect|Prev=0.1|Next=0.22}}{{Collapse|num=2|initial=

|body=
The first thing you notice in '''Level 0.11''' is the smell.

Not the water.

Not the walls.

The smell.

It sits in the air like something old and dying, a mixture of rust, mildew, and something far worse that refuses to leave your lungs once you breathe it in.

I didn't understand it at first.

I thought it was just rot.

But '''Level 0.11''' doesn't rot the way normal places do.

It decays.

Slowly.

Deliberately.

The corridor I entered was half-flooded, the murky water reaching nearly halfway up my boots.

The surface rippled with every movement, reflecting the flickering fluorescent lights above.

The bulbs buzzed constantly, the sound echoing through the endless halls.

Drip.

Drip.

Drip.

Water fell from somewhere above, though the ceiling pipes looked dry.

At least at first.

I had entered from [[Level 0.8]] through a door I barely remember finding.

One moment I was wandering the yellow halls, and the next I was knee-deep in brown water.

There were pipes everywhere.

They ran along the walls in crooked lines, some broken, others leaking slowly into the water.

The metal had turned dark brown with corrosion.

And they vibrated constantly.

A low rumble moved through them like something traveling inside.

Something heavy.

Something slow.

The water around my feet shifted slightly as the pipes groaned.

I kept walking.

Because in '''Level 0.11''', standing still feels worse than moving.

After about twenty minutes of walking, the hallway split.

Then it split again.

And again.

Each intersection looked identical.

Concrete walls.

Rotting carpet beneath the water.

Flickering lights above.

But the corridors didn't behave like normal hallways.

Sometimes they bent in ways they shouldn't.

Sometimes they looped.

Once I turned left and somehow ended up back where I started.

The architecture didn't care about direction.

It rearranged itself whenever it wanted.

The longer you walk, the harder it becomes to remember where you have been.

The water was worse than it looked.

Sometimes it deepened suddenly.

One step ankle-deep.

The next nearly to my knees.

The liquid felt thick.

Not mud.

Something heavier.

Something brushed my boot beneath the surface.

I froze.

Then it drifted away.

Probably debris.

Probably.

The water carries pathogens.

Algae.

Fungi.

Something known as the Hydrolitis Plague.

Thinking about it too long makes the smell worse.

And the smell never leaves.

Eventually the smog rolled in.

Gray and thick against the ceiling.

The air felt heavier.

My head began to ache.

The pipes rattled again.

Something inside them moved.

Then the water shifted.

And for a moment…

The reflection in the water was not mine.

Later I found a dry hallway.

No pipes.

No water.

A Safe Zone.

But the silence there felt worse.

Because without the dripping…

I could hear footsteps.

Slow.

Splashing.

Somewhere behind me.
